pages.title: MCPWP API Reference | WordPress MCP REST Endpoint
h1s: MCPWP API reference for connected agents.

links.text: Endpoint MCP route Connect clients to /wp-json/site-pilot-ai/v1/mcp.
Auth API key header Use X-API-Key with a scoped spai_ key generated in MCPWP.
Clients MCP clients Use the same endpoint pattern for Codex, Claude, Cursor, and Windsurf.

pages.title: MCPWP Blog | WordPress MCP and AI Site Operations
h1s: MCPWP field notes for WordPress AI operations.

links.text: Article Ten Signals That a WordPress AI Plugin Is Ready for Market A market-ready plugin is not just functional. It has clear messaging, visible workflows, safe access, and proof that it matches the buyer’s job.
Article Why the Blog Exists Even When the Product Is the Real Offer The blog is not a side quest. It is where the product explains itself, earns search visibility, and proves operational maturity.
Article The Product Story Behind a WordPress MCP Launcher Page A launcher page must explain the problem, the workflow, and the first successful outcome. Anything else is just decoration.
Article How We Turned SEO Cleanup Into a Product Feature If SEO metadata is stale, the site feels unfinished. We treated cleanup as part of the product experience, not a back-office chore.
Article What a Good MCP Demo Has to Prove Before Anyone Buys A real demo should show the page state, the action, and the verification step. If it skips any of those, it is not convincing.
Article How a Safe WordPress AI Product Earns Agency Trust Agencies buy control. The product earns trust when it separates read-only inspection, scoped writes, and risky admin actions.

Article What We Learned Cleaning Old Brand Copy Out of a Live Launch Site Old plugin names, stale counts, and legacy URLs do not just look messy. They damage trust, SEO coherence, and product credibility.

Article The Commercial Difference Between Fixed Counts and Dynamic Tools Static claims like 'MCPWP tools' age badly. A commercially serious plugin should explain categories, scopes, and live discovery instead.

Article Why We Kept Gutenberg for the Blog and Elementor for Everything Else The blog should behave like an editorial system. The product and funnel pages should behave like a controlled conversion system.

Article How We Turned a WordPress Plugin Into a Real AI Operator The difference between a chatbot wrapper and a commercially viable WordPress MCP product is whether it can actually operate the site safely.
Article MCPWP: Controlled AI Site Operations Through MCP MCPWP connects AI agents to WordPress through scoped API keys, Elementor-aware workflows, dynamic tools, and controlled site operations.
Article How an Ecommerce Store Owner Runs WooCommerce With AI How store owners can use MCPWP to manage products, orders, customers, and revenue workflows through controlled AI tools.

ctas.text: Article What a Good MCP Demo Has to Prove Before Anyone Buys A real demo should show the page state, the action, and the verification step. If it skips any of those, it is not convincing.

Brand Brand consistency Keep AI-generated WordPress pages aligned with a brand canon.

pages.title: MCPWP Getting Started | WordPress MCP Setup
h1s: Set up MCPWP in one read-first session.

links.text: 01 Download Use the live versioned package link so the ZIP is current.
02 Create a key Start with a read-only or tightly scoped key in MCPWP settings.
03 Connect client Add the MCP endpoint to Codex, Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, or Windsurf.
04 Inspect first Ask the AI client to list site state and available MCPWP tools before writes.
